title: Katsuragi Research Expedition What happened
wikitext:
[]: Katsuragi Research Expedition--What happened?             Quote Post by Cosman246 » Sat Oct 22, 2011 7:39 pm This is a discussion thread for speculation on the events leading up to Second Impact on the Katsuragi Research Expedition. My theory was that, at the time, the expedition had broken into groups--one which rejected the Super Solenoid theory (the guy who said "No way, that's far too radical!" in episode 21), one which accepted it without embellishments (led by Katsuragi himself), and one which elaborated upon it (probably with "inspiration" from SEELE's Dead Sea Scrolls) to predict Second Impact (led by Ikari Yui and Rokubungi Gendo). The third group tried to warn the first two, but Katsuragi wouldn't listen, and so Yui, Gendo, and their colleagues hijacked a ship and sailed it back before the tragedy would happen. After this, Yui and Gendo took over, forming GEHIRN.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Oct 22, 2011 7:44 pm The problem with the idea of the third group that tried to warn of the tragedy before it happened is that there's a lot of evidence pointing to the fact that Second Impact was simply allowed to happen, if not designed. It was mighty suspicious that Gendo made it out a day before Second Impact (Yui was not there, as far as I can remember), and Fuyutsuki caught onto how neatly it was timed. Besides, it wasn't the Super Solenoid Theory that caused Second Impact; it was how they were testing the theory (specifically: using a Seed of Life).

[]:             Quote Post by Cosman246 » Sat Oct 22, 2011 7:54 pm Sachi wrote: Besides, it wasn't the Super Solenoid Theory that caused Second Impact; it was how they were testing the theory (specifically: using a Seed of Life).

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Oct 22, 2011 7:59 pm Cosman246 wrote: Sorry, I should have clarified that. However, the "Second Impact allowed to happen" evidence does not contradict the claim--the drama happened beforehand, and so the third group left beforehand

[]:             Quote Post by Cosman246 » Sat Oct 22, 2011 8:05 pm Sachi wrote: Does Gendo really seem like the kind of guy to warn of these things? This was before Gendo was in any position of power, and he was still trying to work his way up with Seele. Would Gendo really risk his life's work to go against Seele's wishes and play Cassandra like you propose?

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Sat Oct 22, 2011 8:38 pm Besides, it wasn't the Super Solenoid Theory that caused Second Impact; it was how they were testing the theory (specifically: using a Seed of Life). Cosman246 wrote: Sorry, I should have clarified that. However, the "Second Impact allowed to happen" evidence does not contradict the claim--the drama happened beforehand, and so the third group left beforehand

[]:             Quote Post by Cosman246 » Sat Oct 22, 2011 8:59 pm 1) Yes. 2) No. 3) Voiceover

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sat Oct 22, 2011 10:38 pm In the end, no one knows exactly what happened because the only survivor (Misato) was too young to know exactly what was going on. The video footage from '21 only gives a very vague indication of Adam running amok before the expedition used the LoL to stop him.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sun Oct 23, 2011 3:01 am thread title wrote: What happened?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Oct 23, 2011 9:31 am Gatotsu911 basically summed it up. Dah munster white jyant jus' asploded! OUUAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA~~~~

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sun Oct 23, 2011 10:34 am Someone should write a Thing-Evangelion crossover story involving the Katsuragi Expedition and Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sun Oct 23, 2011 11:13 am gatotsu911 wrote: They blew up.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Oct 23, 2011 11:24 am Fazmotron wrote: Also, everyone turned into little red dots... If Kurt Russell is in it, then I'm totally all for it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 23, 2011 11:34 am Trajan wrote: Someone should write a Thing-Evangelion crossover story involving the Katsuragi Expedition and Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Oct 23, 2011 3:28 pm Trajan wrote: In the end, no one knows exactly what happened because the only survivor (Misato) was too young to know exactly what was going on. The video footage from '21 only gives a very vague indication of Adam running amok before the expedition used the LoL to stop him.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Oct 23, 2011 3:33 pm Bagheera wrote: Huh. Souls? Never noticed that before. The Expedition's, the Angels', or both?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Oct 23, 2011 3:36 pm Bagheera wrote: Their attempt to use the LoL is why he ran amok (well, it was injecting him with Lilith-based DNA that did it, really, but the Lance was part of the process . . . er, somehow).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Oct 23, 2011 3:42 pm Azathoth wrote: Symbolically speaking, it's pretty obviously what was used to impregnate Lilith with Misato's/Katsuragi's/whoever's DNA.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Oct 23, 2011 3:54 pm Bagheera wrote: Also, Misato was the same age as Shinji and Asuka at the time, FWIW.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Oct 23, 2011 3:57 pm She's 29. remember she was working in Germany with babby Asuka for some time between college and the events of the series
